text.title: Idea Generation (Researcher) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Idea Generation (Researcher) application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Generation starts from pain-point corpus / interview log, NOT from skills inventory. Skills-first seeding is reserved for the market-researcher tier.
rule.rationale: Research-tier ideation must surface evidence-anchored candidates; skills-first biases toward what the operator wants to build.

rule.statement: Generate ≥30 candidates BEFORE scoring any. Mid-session scoring is rejected.
Top-5 candidates MUST link to ≥1 evidence source each (interview, ticket, observed switching). Unlinked top-5 are demoted.
rule.rationale: Top candidates flow into downstream validation; unlinked candidates waste validation budget.

rule.statement: Personal fit <3 overrides any weighted total — drop the candidate.
rule.rationale: Fit determines whether the operator will ship.

rule.statement: Deduplicate candidates by underlying Job-to-be-done, not by name.
rule.rationale: Name-based dedup leaves duplicate jobs and inflates the pool.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
